item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OHHHHHHHHHHHHH!
The ending is incredibly… well it's not a totally twist, but still, breath-taking. Heart-breaking also.
+1
I really hope I can upvote more than one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfect analysis, you've said almost all I think. Wikidot should have a button to upvote a comment.

item.title: Nothing's new
item.content:encoded:
Being incapable of originality…yeah, that stings. One of the scariest things I can think of.

Remarkable work, thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how specific a fear/anxiety this taps into. As well as being (very) dark humor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, Hemingway commited suicide by shooting himself with a shotgun he had painted into existence?

What a strange world we live in!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is what trying to write a new SCP feels like

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad I noticed that! I just pointed that out in a different comment, and then here it is, by the author himself!

Great work, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think she's sad simply because they're not original. She started painting as something to do now that her husband was dead. Founding out that they were, in a way, after-the-fact copies took that special quality from making them.

And notice, this isn't one after the other. There is significant time between most of these. She's not home with her cats. She doesn't have her husband. The Foundation won't let her live her life her own way, other than to do the thing that is simply no longer special to her. She has nothing. So she took a way out, and even that was a copy.

At least, that's how I see it. Still tragic as hell. And in a way that doesn't really fall into the classic SCP tropes. Great stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did she just paint Sanic??

SHE JUST PAINTED SANIC.

I was already going to update with no hesitation. "Barnyard Hijinks." Jesus. This is incredible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's meant to demonstrate the Foundation's increasing lack of interest and her own increasing despair.

They didn't commit years of repeated testing; they just did less and less testing over time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why are all the tests several years apart? I don't see why all of these attempts couldn't have been thought of in a week of testing. I'd hardly think they would have had enough test ideas or repeat tests to justify years of testing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …but at least I got her pen!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, my heart was not prepared for that ending. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Worth a +1 I fink.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can’t emphasis enough the extent to which the ending has utterly torpedoed my mood. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the character’s ineluctable surprise that these works are already made, some of them even famous and revered. Has a wonderful meta irony to it, also in a way because we as authors sometimes feel like she does (Simpsons Series 1 did it).

I like some formatting flairs going on here (bolded brackets at logs begin and end, the use of line breaks in the test logs) so I’m going to adopt them for my articles if you don’t mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending was abrupt, but this was short enough that it didn't outstay its welcome. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a wonderful piece of bleak, absurdist humour, ramping up the laughs until you devastate with a conclusion that was so obvious in hindsight. This is an excellent skip; very strong +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure about the ending, but I really enjoyed the logic of her attempts to make something new.

I was kind of expecting some sort of reference to retro-causality to come up, and I like that it didn't.

item.title: ORIGINAL ART, DONT STEAL
item.content:encoded: The theme of originality in art really shines here to the point of being anvillish, but in a good way. That ending is perfectly tragic too. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I don't feel this brings anything new to the table to the 'otherwise ordinary person is traumatised by imprisonment' script. The parallel point about artistic imitation is different but it exists alongside that script instead of adding to it.

The idea that this is derivative as a metajoke is clever but doesn't make it more interesting to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i really like this. something about the ending is just so bleak and sudden.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Specifically, fan-art of Sonic the Hedgehog, a video-game character produced by Sega in 1991.

Hippo, you absolute madlad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember way back in the day when Mendelssohn was pitching an article like this and I was exceptionally iffy about it

I still am, sadly. It just feels too predictable, honestly.

Edit: It feels too predictable but even predictable articles can be written in an endearing way. Unfortunately, this just felt really one-note and I knew exactly where the testing logs would go.

Edit 2: Unless there's a meta moment of this SCP already exists and you made it a second time to show that they really can't even do anything uniquely, and if that's true then okay, fine, that'd be an upvote.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Now go learn to live
The legend you've become
There is nothing new under the sun

There is nothing new under the sun
There is nothing new

SOURCES:

  • Image here.

Like the article? Click here for more of The Great Hippo's Skippos!
